title: Door lock control experiment (15 subjects, 4 classes, 31 EEG ch)
description: A P300-based brain-computer interface dataset for home appliance control, comprising EEG recordings from 15 healthy subjects performing a visual oddball task with door lock control commands. The dataset includes 31-channel EEG data sampled at 500 Hz, with target and non-target stimulus classifications across 50 training and 30 testing trials per subject, designed to evaluate BCI performance in real-world home automation applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 31-channel actiCHamp system (Brain Products) at 500 Hz sampling rate with linked mastoid reference and active sensors. Participants performed a visual P300 oddball paradigm with 750 ms stimulus onset asynchrony, viewing flash stimuli on an LCD display for door lock control. Each subject completed one session with 50 training and 30 testing blocks, with trial duration of 1.0 s and visual feedback provided online.
